kubernetes.core.helm.short_description: Manages Kubernetes packages with the Helm package manager

kubernetes.core.helm_info.short_description: Get information from Helm package deployed inside the cluster

kubernetes.core.helm_pull.short_description: download a chart from a repository and (optionally) unpack it in local directory.
kubernetes.core.helm_pull.description: Retrieve a package from a package repository, and download it locally. It can also be used to perform cryptographic verification of a chart without installing the chart. There are options for unpacking the chart after download.

kubernetes.core.helm_template.description: Render chart templates to an output directory or as text of concatenated yaml documents.

kubernetes.core.k8s_cluster_info.short_description: Describe Kubernetes (K8s) cluster, APIs available and their respective versions
kubernetes.core.k8s_cluster_info.description: Use the Kubernetes Python client to perform read operations on K8s objects. Authenticate using either a config file, certificates, password or token. Supports check mode.

invalidate_cache.description: Invalidate cache before retrieving information about cluster.

kubernetes.core.k8s_cp.short_description: Copy files and directories to and from pod.
kubernetes.core.k8s_cp.description: Use the Kubernetes Python client to copy files and directories to and from containers inside a pod.

kubernetes.core.k8s_drain.short_description: Drain, Cordon, or Uncordon node in k8s cluster
kubernetes.core.k8s_drain.description: Drain node in preparation for maintenance same as kubectl drain. Cordon will mark the node as unschedulable. Uncordon will mark the node as schedulable. The given node will be marked unschedulable to prevent new pods from arriving. Then drain deletes all pods except mirror pods (which cannot be deleted through the API server).

state.description: Determines whether to drain, cordon, or uncordon node.

pod_selectors.description: Label selector to filter pods on the node. This option has effect only when C(state) is set to I(drain).

delete_options.description: Specify options to delete pods. This option has effect only when C(state) is set to I(drain).

kubernetes.core.k8s_json_patch.short_description: Apply JSON patch operations to existing objects
kubernetes.core.k8s_json_patch.description: This module is used to apply RFC 6902 JSON patch operations only. Use the M(kubernetes.core.k8s) module for strategic merge or JSON merge operations. The jsonpatch library is required for check mode.

api_version.description: Use to specify the API version. Use in conjunction with I(kind), I(name), and I(namespace) to identify a specific object.

hidden_fields.description: List of fields to hide from the diff output. This is useful for fields that are not relevant to the patch operation, such as `metadata.managedFields`.

kind.description: Use to specify an object model. Use in conjunction with I(api_version), I(name), and I(namespace) to identify a specific object.

namespace.description: Use to specify an object namespace. Use in conjunction with I(api_version), I(kind), and I(name) to identify a specific object.

name.description: Use to specify an object name. Use in conjunction with I(api_version), I(kind), and I(namespace) to identify a specific object.

kubernetes.core.k8s_scale.short_description: Set a new size for a Deployment, ReplicaSet, Replication Controller, or Job.

kubernetes.core.k8s_service.description: Use Kubernetes Python SDK to manage Services on Kubernetes

merge_type.description: Whether to override the default patch merge approach with a specific type. By default, the strategic merge will typically be used. For example, Custom Resource Definitions typically aren

kubernetes.core.k8s_taint.short_description: Taint a node in a Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster
kubernetes.core.k8s_taint.description: Taint allows a node to refuse Pod to be scheduled unless that Pod has a matching toleration. Untaint will remove taints from nodes as needed.

state.description: Determines whether to add or remove taints.

replace.description: If C(true), allow taints to be replaced.
